steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The first sentence states that Leda orbits Jupiter in the same direction Jupiter rotates. The second sentence names the label for that orbital direction: “prograde.” The label is a direct result of the orbital fact — the relationship is cause to consequence.

steps.title: Select the transition that signals consequence
steps.content:
Only a consequence transition fits between a cause and its named result.
  • Choice A — In reality,: signals a correction of a mistaken assumption. No assumption is being corrected; the second sentence simply names an outcome. ✗
  • Choice B — Nevertheless,: signals contrast despite an obstacle. Receiving the label “prograde” is not a surprising or contrary outcome. ✗
  • Choice C — Hence,: signals that the second sentence is the logical consequence of the first, fitting the cause-effect relationship exactly. ✓
  • Choice D — Next,: signals the next step in a time sequence. The passage explains a logical consequence, not a sequence of events. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
